ontology.foodon_gap_note: FoodOn has a generic pancake food product class; no specific durian pancake or durian crepe entry exists. Mapped to closest parent class.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 code A0FKP covers pancakes and crepes as a category. No durian-filled crepe-specific code exists; this is the closest defensible anchor.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No SNOMED CT code for durian pancake or filled crepe snack identified in the clinical terminology. Left null to preserve ontology honesty.

serving.natural_language_portion: One durian pancake piece typically weighs approximately 90 g and consists of a thin crepe wrapper enclosing a generous filling of fresh durian pulp and whipped cream.

nutrient_source.title: Durian Pancake — Singapore HPB Foodo Nutrient Composition Database
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Nutrient values sourced directly from HPB Singapore Foodo database entry for Durian Pancake (cr_id F11100003). All core macros and secondary nutrients are taken from this canonical entry with confidence 0.95.

gi_source.title: Glycemic Index of Pancake and Crepe Products — analogous estimate based on wheat-flour crepe with sweet filling
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement for durian pancake (filled crepe) exists in the University of Sydney GI Database. GI of 49 is estimated from the closest analogous entry: thin wheat-flour crepe with sweet dairy-based filling, which falls in the low-GI range (GI ~47–52) per University of Sydney GI Database records for pancake and crepe products. The durian pulp contributes natural fructose and sucrose; the high dietary fibre content (4.6 g/100g) moderates the glycaemic response. Glycemic load per serving calculated as: (GI × carbohydrate_g per serving) / 100 = (49 × 23.4) / 100 ≈ 11.4 for a 90 g serving (carbohydrate_g per serving = 26.0 × 0.90 = 23.4 g). Input record GI confidence was 0.643, consistent with an analogous estimate.

hypertension.key_factor: Sugar 12.1 g/100g exceeds the 10 g/100g solid-food threshold; insulin-mediated sodium retention risk per AHA 2018 guidelines despite low sodium (57 mg/100g)
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should limit durian pancake to one piece (~90 g) per occasion and monitor overall daily sugar intake, as the 12.1 g/100g sugar content can drive insulin-mediated sodium retention even when dietary sodium is low.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Total carbohydrate 26 g/100g with sugar 12.1 g/100g from durian fructose and sucrose; GI 49 (low) partially mitigated by 4.6 g/100g dietary fibre
type2_diabetes.guidance: People with Type 2 Diabetes should restrict durian pancake to one piece per sitting, pair it with a low-carbohydrate meal, and monitor postprandial blood glucose, as the combined carbohydrate and natural sugar load from durian pulp can elevate glycaemic response despite the low GI.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Saturated fat 2.8 g/100g (below 5 g/100g threshold), trans fat 0 g, sodium 57 mg/100g (low), and dietary fibre 4.6 g/100g (cardioprotective)
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Durian pancake presents a low cardiovascular concern due to its low saturated fat (2.8 g/100g), absence of trans fat, low sodium (57 mg/100g), and meaningful dietary fibre (4.6 g/100g); occasional consumption of one piece is acceptable within a heart-healthy diet, though the whipped cream filling should be confirmed as free of partially hydrogenated oils.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: No trans fat, saturated fat below 5 g/100g, fibre 4.6 g/100g (anti-inflammatory), vitamin C 5 mg/100g from durian flesh
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: Durian pancake is generally suitable for individuals with rheumatoid arthritis when limited to one piece, as its low saturated fat, absence of trans fat, high fibre, and vitamin C from durian pulp support an anti-inflammatory dietary pattern; however, the 12.1 g/100g sugar content should be monitored to avoid pro-inflammatory effects.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Durian pancake (榴莲班戟 / 榴蓮班戟) is a popular Singaporean snack consisting of a thin, soft wheat-flour crepe wrapper folded around a generous filling of fresh durian pulp and lightly sweetened whipped cream. The crepe is pan-fried to a pale golden colour, then cooled before being filled and folded into a neat rectangular or pillow shape. The texture contrasts a delicate, slightly chewy crepe exterior with a rich, custardy, and intensely aromatic durian interior. Durian pancakes are widely sold at hawker centres, dessert shops, and pasar malam (night markets) across Singapore and Malaysia, and are especially popular as an afternoon snack or festive treat during durian season. Nutritionally, each 100 g provides 166 kcal, 4.2 g protein, 4.97 g fat (saturated fat 2.8 g, no trans fat), 26 g carbohydrate, 12.1 g sugar, 4.6 g dietary fibre, and 57 mg sodium. The high dietary fibre content (4.6 g/100g) is notable for a snack item and derives primarily from the durian pulp. The GI is estimated at 49 (low), yielding a glycaemic load of approximately 11.4 per 90 g serving. Clinically, the moderate sugar content (12.1 g/100g) warrants portion control for individuals managing Type 2 Diabetes or hypertension, while the low saturated fat and absence of trans fat make it a relatively heart-friendly occasional snack. The vitamin C contribution (~5 mg/100g) from durian flesh provides antioxidant benefit relevant to inflammatory conditions such as rheumatoid arthritis.
common_user_queries: How many calories in a durian pancake?
Is durian pancake suitable for hypertension?
What is the GI of durian pancake?
How much sugar is in a durian pancake?
Is durian pancake high in fibre?
Where to buy durian pancake in Singapore?

confidence_reason: Core nutrient values sourced directly from the HPB Singapore Foodo database (cr_id F11100003) with input confidence 0.95. All macros and secondary nutrients are from this canonical approved primary source. GI is an analogous estimate from the University of Sydney GI Database (confidence 0.643 per input, consistent with analogous estimate classification). Multilingual names are well-established for this widely sold Singaporean snack.
notes: 1. monounsaturated_fat_g and polyunsaturated_fat_g set to -1: detailed fatty acid breakdown for durian pancake not reported in the HPB Singapore Foodo database entry F11100003 as of 2026-04-16. 2. trans_fat_g set to 0.0: confirmed negligible/absent per HPB source and consistent with the health_suitability input noting no trans fat. 3. potassium_mg, cholesterol_mg, vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg, phosphorus_mg, magnesium_mg, zinc_mg, selenium_mcg, water_g set to -1: these micronutrient and moisture values are not reported in the HPB Foodo database entry for this food item as of 2026-04-16. 4. vitamin_c_mg set to 5.0 mg/100g: derived from the input health_suitability rheumatoid_arthritis note citing vitamin C (5 mg/100g) from durian flesh; this is consistent with published durian pulp composition data in USDA FoodData Central (durian raw, FDC ID 168192, ~19.7 mg/100g for pure pulp, diluted by crepe and cream components). 5. saturated_fat_g set to 2.8 g/100g: derived from the input health_suitability rheumatoid_arthritis note citing saturated fat at 2.8 g/100g; consistent with a crepe-and-cream product at this fat level. 6. GI of 49 is an analogous estimate from University of Sydney GI Database (wheat-flour crepe with sweet filling); input GI confidence was 0.643. Glycemic load per 90 g serving = (49 × 23.4) / 100 = 11.4. 7. Halal status assessed as likely halal (no pork or alcohol ingredients); formal halal certification should be verified with the specific vendor.
